sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the last image (STEP 19), the top rectangle is purple and the bottom is white. But the reference shows white on top and purple on bottom. Something is still wrong.
Let me check the current state of the rectangles to understand what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state of rectangles
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
rects = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 50 and n['color'] in ('W', 'p', 'P'):
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [b[0] for b in boundary]
        cols = [b[1] for b in boundary]
        center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
        center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
        rects.append((center_row, n['color'], n['pixels']))
        print(f"  center=({center_row}, {center_col}), color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
rects.sort()
print(f"Top rect: {rects[0]}")
print(f"Bottom rect: {rects[1]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
center=(28, 31), color=p, pixels=84
  center=(38, 31), color=W, pixels=100
Top rect: (28, 'p', 84)
Bottom rect: (38, 'W', 100)

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Top is purple (p), bottom is white (W). The reference shows white on top, purple on bottom. So I need to swap them again. But my previous 4-action batch didn't work correctly. Let me try again, but this time let me be more careful about the order and make sure each paint action actually takes effect.
Let me try doing them one at a time and checking after each.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, paint top rectangle white
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 28, 'col': 31},  # select top rectangle
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 4, 'col': 37},    # paint white
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
